title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
summary: A system design tutorial that teaches a 7-step framework for approaching design problems: requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack, and implementation constraints. The author argues that most tutorials wrongly start with technology rather than understanding the problem first.
writeup: This tutorial from AWS Developers lays out a 7-step framework for working through system design problems: requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack, and implementation constraints. The central argument is that most tutorials start with technology, when the problem itself should come first. The framework also folds in failure analysis alongside traffic estimation and tech stack selection, positioning problem understanding ahead of tooling decisions.
